# SaltGrass Modular

Custom modular construction company specializing in container homes, modular pools, traditional modular builds, and rapid-deployment housing for homeowners, developers, and government/military.

## About
- Founder: Dylan Walker
- HQ: Oklahoma City, Oklahoma (40,000+ sq ft fabrication facility)
- Serves: United States nationwide (Oklahoma is the home state; surrounding region is the routine delivery radius; nationwide for larger projects and government/military)

## Services
- Custom Container Homes — /services/container-homes
- Traditional Modular Builds — /services/traditional-builds
- Modular Pools — /services/pools
- Disaster Relief & Rapid Deployment — /services/disaster-relief
- Developers & Military Solutions — /services/developers

- Home — /

- Service areas (states + metros) — /locations

- Financing — /financing
- Blog — /blog
- Contact — /contact

## Quick-Answer Facts
- What does a container home cost? Starting prices (all-in: fabrication + delivery + installation): Studio 320 SF — $75,000; 1-Bedroom 640 SF — $105,000; 2-Bedroom 960 SF — $155,000.
- What do modular pools cost? Tube Steel + Fiberglass — $12,000–$24,000; Welded Steel + Epoxy — $18,000–$36,000 (installed).
- How long does a build take? About 8–9 months from design to install (factory fabrication runs in parallel with site prep and permitting).
- Is it tornado/hurricane resistant? Steel-framed modular and container structures are engineered to the wind-load requirements of each specific site.
- How does permitting work? Requirements vary by jurisdiction; SaltGrass coordinates with your local Authority Having Jurisdiction (AHJ). We do not assert specific statutes, which differ by county and city.
- Are modular and manufactured (mobile) homes the same? No — modular homes are built to the same local building codes as site-built homes and placed on permanent foundations.
